name: Chainlink SmartCon 2025
edition: SmartCon 2025 (annual flagship Chainlink conference; first return to New York City since 2022)
location: Metropolitan Pavilion, Manhattan, New York City, USA
attendance: ~1,700 in-person attendees (reported; the precise '1,701 confirmed guests' figure came from a Vendelux attendee-list page now returning HTTP 410 Gone and is no longer independently verifiable — secondary sources cite only 'thousands of attendees'); Chainlink cited 275+ speakers, thousands of registrations, and millions of online views. (Note: the official agenda page lists overlapping session times across multiple stages, consistent with a multi-track event.)
defi_themes: TradFi-DeFi convergence ('AllFi') and institutional adoption of onchain finance
Real-world asset (RWA) tokenization (funds, securities, treasuries) at institutional scale
Stablecoins for payments and as a financial-stack primitive
Institutional DeFi lending and money markets (Aave Horizon, tokenized-asset collateral)
Onchain compliance as infrastructure (Chainlink ACE + 20+ compliance providers)
Cross-chain interoperability and settlement via CCIP (DvP, cross-chain lending, liquidity)
Derivatives and perpetuals (the 'perp economy', Hyperliquid, Injective)
Liquid staking and restaking (LSTs, restaking security for BTC)
Bitcoin DeFi / BTCFi (SolvBTC, Lombard interchain Bitcoin)
Onchain yield and next-gen asset management (asset managers going onchain)
Proof of Reserve and trusted onchain data / pricing & NAV (DataLink)
Privacy for institutional DeFi (Chainlink Confidential Compute)
Regulation and the U.S. digital-asset policy landscape ('From Crypto to Capitol Hill')

key_takeaways: TradFi-DeFi convergence was the dominant narrative: major institutions (UBS, Swift, JPMorgan/Kinexys, Mastercard, Fidelity, Wellington, WisdomTree, Invesco, VanEck) showcased production onchain finance, framed as 'AllFi'.
Chainlink positioned the Chainlink Runtime Environment (CRE) as the all-in-one orchestration layer to connect TradFi, DeFi, and tokenized assets, presented as live for institutional-grade workflows.

Compliance is becoming embedded infrastructure: the Automated Compliance Engine (ACE) launched a 20+ provider partner ecosystem (incl. Chainalysis cross-chain compliance workflows), with Aave Horizon adopting ACE for institutional lending against tokenized assets.

RWA tokenization moved from pilots to production: UBS demonstrated a first end-to-end production tokenized fund workflow using a Digital Transfer Agent standard with Chainlink; Securitize, Ondo, Superstate, VanEck and others featured heavily.
Stablecoins were treated as a core primitive for payments and the broader financial stack, with multiple dedicated 'how to create a stablecoin' and payments sessions.
Cross-chain settlement (Delivery-vs-Payment) between public and private chains via CCIP was a recurring institutional theme (WisdomTree, JPMorgan Kinexys, BX Digital).

DeFi-native verticals were well represented: perpetuals/derivatives (Injective, Hyperliquid), liquid staking/restaking (stake.link, Solv/Symbiotic), Bitcoin DeFi (Lombard, SolvBTC), and onchain yield (Katana, GSR).
Privacy was flagged as a key institutional adoption barrier; Chainlink Confidential Compute was announced (Early Access early 2026).
U.S. policy/regulation was front-and-center under the 'From Crypto to Capitol Hill' theme, including a White House Digital Assets Council speaker.
Proof of Reserve and trusted onchain data (DataLink, S&P Global pricing/NAV) were emphasized as the trust layer for collateralization of RWAs and stablecoins.
